taxonomy: Amoria tomentosa belongs to the family of plants known as Fabaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants often referred to as legumes. It is a part of the subfamily Faboideae and further classified under the tribe Millettieae. The species Amoria tomentosa is native to tropical regions and is characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. The plant is further categorized under the genus Amoria, which is a relatively small group of plants.
